[Home](./index.md) &gt; [@formspec/constraints](./constraints.md)

## constraints package

@<!-- -->formspec/constraints

Constraint validation for FormSpec - restrict features based on target environment capabilities.

This package provides: - Type definitions for constraint configuration - YAML/TypeScript config file loading - Core validation logic for FormSpec elements - JSON Schema for .formspec.yml validation

## Example


```ts
import { loadConfig, validateFormSpecElements } from '@formspec/constraints';
import { formspec, field } from '@formspec/dsl';

// Load constraints from .formspec.yml
const { config } = await loadConfig();

// Create a form
const form = formspec(
  field.text("name"),
  field.dynamicEnum("country", "countries"),
);

// Validate against constraints
const result = validateFormSpecElements(form.elements, { constraints: config });

if (!result.valid) {
  console.error('Validation failed:', result.issues);
}
```

[validateFormSpecElements(elements, options)](./constraints.validateformspecelements.md)


</td><td>

Validates FormSpec elements against constraints.

This is the main entry point for validating a form specification against a constraint configuration. It walks through all elements and checks each one against the configured constraints.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[validateLayout(context, constraints)](./constraints.validatelayout.md)


</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Validates a layout element against constraints.


</td></tr>

Interface

Description

<tbody><tr><td>

[ArrayField](./constraints.arrayfield.md)


</td><td>

An array field containing repeating items.

Use this for lists of values (e.g., multiple addresses, line items).

A boolean checkbox field.

Complete constraint configuration for a FormSpec project.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ControlOptionConstraints](./constraints.controloptionconstraints.md)


</td><td>

Control options constraints - control which JSONForms Control.options are allowed. These are renderer-specific options that may not be universally supported.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[DynamicEnumField](./constraints.dynamicenumfield.md)


</td><td>

A field with dynamic enum options (fetched from a data source at runtime).


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[DynamicSchemaField](./constraints.dynamicschemafield.md)


</td><td>

A field that loads its schema dynamically (e.g., from an extension).


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[EnumOption](./constraints.enumoption.md)


</td><td>

An enum option with a separate ID and display label.

Use this when the stored value (id) should differ from the display text (label).


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FieldOptionConstraints](./constraints.fieldoptionconstraints.md)

Field configuration option constraints - control which field options are allowed.

**_(BETA)_** Context for field option validation.

**_(BETA)_** Context for field type validation.

A complete form specification.

[StaticEnumField](./constraints.staticenumfield.md)

A field with static enum options (known at compile time).

[DEFAULT\_CONSTRAINTS](./constraints.default_constraints.md)

<tr><td>

</td><td>

Union of all form element types (fields and structural elements).


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[Severity](./constraints.severity.md)


</td><td>

Severity level for constraint violations. - "error": Violation fails validation - "warn": Violation emits warning but passes - "off": Feature is allowed (no violation)


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
